findings.issue: Fetch call to /notify endpoint lacks await and error handling, causing untracked promise rejection and potential resource leak.
findings.suggestion: Either await the fetch or add explicit .catch() to prevent silent failures and log errors to ARCH_LOG.
findings.est_impact: Multiple unhandled promise rejections accumulate; memory waste on abandoned fetch operations.

findings.issue: Second fetch call to /notify (line 271) also lacks await, creating identical resource leak pattern.
findings.suggestion: Consolidate both notification fetches into a single helper function with proper error logging.
findings.est_impact: Repeated resource leaks multiply with each high-severity scan result.

findings.issue: Date string slicing `new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10)` appears 3 times (lines 74, 305).
findings.suggestion: Extract as a utility function `function getToday() { return new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10); }`.
findings.est_impact: Minor code maintenance burden; negligible token cost but poor style.

findings.issue: REJECTED constant (line 18) and PROPOSALS directory are initialized but REJECTED is never written to or read from anywhere in the code.
findings.suggestion: Either implement rejection tracking logic or remove the unused constant and related file paths.
findings.est_impact: Dead constant adds confusion and maintains unused file path.

findings.issue: JSON.parse on line 225 within try-catch silently discards parse errors; malformed ARCH_LOG entries corrupt lastEntry without logging.
findings.suggestion: Log parse failures to archLog before catching, or validate JSONL structure on write.
findings.est_impact: Silent data corruption makes debugging zero-streak logic failures harder.
